themes: Manager-heavy backlog with urgent follow-up needed: nearly all 87 contacts are Managers, many waiting well over 100 days, signaling a systemic gap in how SOCi closes the loop with day-to-day power users who drive product adoption on the ground.

Multi-location franchise and property management verticals dominate: Travel Leaders Group, Berkshire Hathaway HomeServices, Holiday Inn, Anytime Fitness, Ford, Avenue5, and Balfour Beatty appear multiple times, suggesting these enterprise segments have unresolved, recurring workflow pain points worth addressing as cohorts.

Wait times signal prioritization and capacity risk: with contacts ranging from 11 to 363 days waiting, the backlog reveals both a triage problem and a trust risk — longer-tenured accounts may be disengaging, making re-engagement sequencing and a clear product response cadence essential.
